_what: FORM RULES as DATA (C3.4) — the validator reads these; adding a law = adding a row, zero code.

rules.description: Tim's law: never give time estimates (hours/days/weeks/ETAs), ever.
rules.feedback: FORM VIOLATION (no-time-estimates): the draft contains a time estimate. Tim's standing law: never give time estimates. Rewrite the response without any duration/ETA language.

rules.description: Tim's law: never claim done/complete/fixed/working without verification evidence.
rules.feedback: FORM VIOLATION (completion-claims-need-evidence): the draft claims completion without verification evidence (no 'verified/observed/tested' nearby). Tim's standing law: completion claims require execution evidence. Either add the observed evidence or soften the claim.
